100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 3, 1926, the British Empire faced mounting tensions as the General Strike continued, with workers demanding better wages and working conditions. In the United States, the investigation into the Teapot Dome scandal intensified, bringing further scrutiny on former Secretary of the Interior Albert Fall. Meanwhile, in Germany, political instability grew as the Weimar Republic grappled with economic challenges and rising extremist movements.
